title: fraxinus × veltheimii
intro: Fraxinus × veltheimii, also known as Veltheim's ash or Fraxinus veltheimii, is a deciduous tree hybrid derived from the Fraxinus excelsior (European ash) and Fraxinus angustifolia (narrow-leaved ash) species. The bark and leaves of this tree have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Fraxinus × veltheimii is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and treating wounds and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of this tree, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
